coredata.dc:title: One-step preparation and dual mode luminescence anti-counterfeiting application of LiYF4: Yb3+, Ho3+, Ce3+@ carbon quantum dot composite materials
coredata.dc:creator: Lü, Jiantao
Liang, Jie
Fan, Ting
Zhou, Yayun
Deng, Tingting
Li, Wenqiang
coredata.dc:description: In the field of fluorescent anti-counterfeiting, materials with single luminous mode are easy to be forged, which cannot meet the current anti-counterfeiting requirements. Currently reported bimodal light-emitting materials usually require multi-step and tedious synthesis steps. In this paper, LiYF4: Yb3+, Ho3+, Ce3+ upconversion micron particles (UCMPs)/carbon quantum dots (CDs) composite materials were prepared by one-step hydrothermal method, which greatly simplified the composite process of CDs and UCMPs. The upconversion wavelength (from green light to red light excited by 980 nm) can be achieved by changing the doping concentration of Ce3+ ions. Through the combination of CDs and UCMPs, an obvious dual mode luminescence phenomenon was achieved. Finally, screen printing was used to produce a clover pattern, which realized multi-color luminescence under 365 nm and 980 nm excitations respectively. The new fluorescence anti-counterfeiting composites, which uses simple and environmentally friendly one-step synthesis process, is expected to promote the development of fluorescence anti-counterfeiting field.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Upconversion
Fluoride
Carbon quantum dots
Dual-mode luminescence
Fluorescent anti-counterfeiting

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work is supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 51702051) and Foshan inorganic micro-nano luminescent materials engineering technology research center.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: X-ray diffraction patterns of the LiYF4: 20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, X% Ce3+ (X = 0, 4, 8, 12, 16)@CDs composites.
(a) TEM images, (b) the corresponding HRTEM images and (c) EDS elemental mapping of LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+ @CDs.
(a) XPS spectrum and (b–c) C 1s and O 1s high-resolution XPS spectrum of the LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+@CDs.
FT-IR spectra of LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+ and LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+@CDs.
Upconversion emission spectra of the LiYF4:Yb3+/Ho3+/Ce3+ (20/2/x%)@CDs (b) CIE chromaticity value diagram of the LiYF4:Yb3+/Ho3+/Ce3+ (20/2/x%)@CDs.
Energy level diagram of Ce3+, Ho3+ and Yb3+ ions and the possible upconversion process.
(a) Excitation under λem = 449 nm and (b) emission spectra of LiYF4:Yb3+/Ho3+/Ce3+ (20/2/8%)@CDs excited by 365 nm.
The photographs of graphic security patterns made with LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, X%Ce (X = 0, 4, 16) @CDs under 365 nm and 980 nm irradiation for dual-mode anti-counterfeiting.
The schematic diagram of LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+@CDs composite.

head.ce:title: One-step preparation and dual mode luminescence anti-counterfeiting application of LiYF4: Yb3+, Ho3+, Ce3+@ carbon quantum dot composite materials

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Hydrogen Energy

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In the field of fluorescent anti-counterfeiting, materials with single luminous mode are easy to be forged, which cannot meet the current anti-counterfeiting requirements. Currently reported bimodal light-emitting materials usually require multi-step and tedious synthesis steps. In this paper, LiYF4: Yb3+, Ho3+, Ce3+ upconversion micron particles (UCMPs)/carbon quantum dots (CDs) composite materials were prepared by one-step hydrothermal method, which greatly simplified the composite process of CDs and UCMPs. The upconversion wavelength (from green light to red light excited by 980 nm) can be achieved by changing the doping concentration of Ce3+ ions. Through the combination of CDs and UCMPs, an obvious dual mode luminescence phenomenon was achieved. Finally, screen printing was used to produce a clover pattern, which realized multi-color luminescence under 365 nm and 980 nm excitations respectively. The new fluorescence anti-counterfeiting composites, which uses simple and environmentally friendly one-step synthesis process, is expected to promote the development of fluorescence anti-counterfeiting field.

Prime Novelty StatementThe prepared LiYF4: Yb3+, Ho3+, Ce3+ upconversion micron particles (UCMPs)/carbon quantum dots (CDs) composite materials had nice luminescence properties, low toxicity. By controlling the Ce3+ ion doping concentration, the gradual change of up conversion green light to red light emission of LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+ microcrystals was realized. The composite materials have different luminescence responses under 365 nm and 985 nm light excitation, respectively.The anti-counterfeiting pattern was prepared and excellent dual mode luminescence anti-counterfeiting phenomenon was obtained.
Image 1

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Rare earth (RE) ion doped fluorescent materials, such as oxides, phosphates, fluorides, vanadates, sulfides, and oxyhalides, have attracted great attention from researchers due to their unique 4f electronic configurations and their wide applications in solid-state lasers, lighting, and anti-counterfeiting labeling [1–3]. Among them, rare earth doped fluorides have been studied for decades due to their unique luminescence, ferromagnetism, insulation, and piezoelectric properties, which have been widely used in laser equipment, 3D flat panel displays, and low intensity infrared imaging [4,5]. LiREF4, as an important class of rare earth fluorides in AREF4 (A = alkali metal, RE = rare earth) materials, can reduce the thermal disturbance effect under strong pumping conditions because of its low phonon energy, high crystallinity and less surface defects, which is especially suitable to be used as upconversion luminescence host of lanthanide ions [6,7]. Yan et al. [6] used tetragonal LiErF4 as active core and prepared an inert epitaxial LiYF4 shell with appropriate thickness to fine-tune the surface properties. Colorimetric sensing nanomaterials with red and green upconversion emission were developed under 1530 nm excitation.

Carbon dots (CDs) have attracted widespread attention and interest in security applications such as anti-counterfeiting, information encryption, and fingerprint recognition due to their wide range of preparation materials, outstanding optical properties, ease of preparation and structural adjustment, good photostability, environmental friendliness, low toxicity, and low cost. They have shown enormous potential in carbon based multifunctional nanomaterials [8–11].

The above two materials have been widely used in traditional anti-counterfeiting materials, but a single luminescent mode is easily falsified. In recent years, researchers have been committed to combining two or more materials with excellent performance to achieve the effect of multimodal luminescence. The combination of CDs and rare earth luminescent materials has attracted increasing attention due to their dual mode optical properties. Li et al. [12] synthesized NaYF4:Er,Yb(Tm) nanocrystals by solvothermal method, dispersed the nanocrystals in the precursor solution for preparing CDs, and finally prepared the composites by hydrothermal method. Due to the introduction of CDs on the surface of rare earth doped upconversion micron particles (UCMP), the composite has the luminescence characteristics of upconversion green light and downconversion blue light at the same time, which is a promising anti-counterfeiting luminescence material. Recently, Shu et al. [13] first synthesized highly luminescent GdVO4:Eu3+ nanocrystals by hydrothermal method, then prepared CDs by hydrothermal method, and then mixed the two to prepare nanocomposites by the third hydrothermal process. The nanocomposites showed red fluorescence under 254 nm excitation, blue fluorescence under 365 nm excitation, and purple fluorescence under simultaneous excitation of two wavelengths. The multiple luminescence modes provide a new method for the field of fluorescence anti-counterfeiting. Zhuang et al. [14] prepared CDs with a large number of negative ion carboxylate groups on the surface by solvothermal method, and then prepared NaYF4:Yb,Er rare earth upconversion luminescent nanoparticles (UCNP) with positively charged amino groups on the surface by solvothermal method. Finally, NaYF4:Yb,Er@CDs nanocomposites were rapidly obtained by physical mixing method, and the anti-counterfeiting patterns of dual-mode luminescence were obtained under the excitation of 365 nm and 980 nm light, respectively.

The reported composite method of CDs and rare earth ion doped micro/nano particles usually requires multi-step synthesis, is cumbersome, time-consuming, has weak stability, and does not appear variable emissions. The simple synthesis method and color adjustable properties of composites needs further research. In this paper, LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+ and CDs composite with LiYF4 as the matrix was successfully prepared by one-step hydrothermal method for the first time. The upconversion luminescence wavelength regulation (from green to red emission excited by 980 nm) can be achieved by changing the Ce3+ ions doping concentration, which realized multi color adjustable luminescence performance. While realizing dual-mode luminescence, the problem of aggregation quenching of solid-state fluorescence caused by the close π-π superposition interaction of CDs is improved. Finally, the anti-counterfeiting pattern of clover is prepared with the composite by screen printing.

Ln(NO3)3·6H2O (Ln = Yb, Ce, Ho, Y) was purchased from Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). LiCl and Lithium citrate tribasic tetrahydrate were purchased from Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). Ethylene glycol (EG, 99%), and NH4F (96%) were purchased from Tianjin Yongda Chemical Reagent Company Limited (Tianjin, China). Oleic acid was purchased from Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). UV curable adhesive was purchased from Shengzhenshi Tegu New Materials CO., LTD.

The preparation process of LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+@CDs composite is as follows. 1 mmol of a certain proportion of rare earth nitrate, 2 mmol of Lithium citrate tribasic tetrahydrate, 2.88 mmol of lithium chloride, and 6 mmol of ammonia fluoride were dissolved in deionized water, followed by 5 ml of oleic acid and 5 ml of ethylene glycol. The mixture was stirred for 30 min, transferred to a 50 ml hydrothermal kettle, and reacted at 180 °C for 6 h. The reactants were centrifuged and washed with ethanol for three times, dried at 80 °C for 6 h and collected for standby. The specific synthesis process is shown in Scheme 1 . The preparation process of LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+ is the same as above but without Lithium citrate tribasic tetrahydrate and ethylene glycol.

Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) spectra of the samples were taken by a IRAffinity-1s Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectrometer (Shimadzu, Japan). The phase composition was recorded by Powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) using a D-Max2500 X-ray diffractometer (XRD, Rigaku, Co., Japan). Down-conversion luminescence spectrums of the samples were recorded on a FLS920P Edinburgh Analytical Instrument. Transmission electron microscope (TEM) (JEM-F200) was used to characterize the morphology and structure of samples at 120 KV. The upconversion luminescence spectra were recorded by a FLS1000 Photoluminescence Spectrometer, Edinburgh Instruments Ltd. ZF-2 ultraviolet analyzer and LE-LS-980-3 (980 nm) (Shenzhen Optoelectronic Technology Co., Ltd.) are used as excitation light sources for anti-counterfeiting patterns 365 nm and 980 nm respectively. The anti-counterfeiting pictures were taken with Canon M100.

The X-ray powder diffraction peaks of the prepared LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+@CDs particles with different Ce3+ ion doping amounts are shown in Fig. 1 . It can be seen that when the doping amount of Ce3+ ions is less than 16%, the prepared particles are consistent with the standard diffraction peak of tetragonal LiYF4 (PDF #77–0816), and the introduction of Yb3+ and Ho3+ ions does not affect the crystal structure. When the doping amount of Ce3+ increases to 16%, some diffraction peaks belonging to YF3 phase are generated, which is similar to previous literature reports [15,16].

The TEM, HRTEM images and EDS elemental mapping of LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+ @CDs are shown in Fig. 2 . According to Fig. 2 (a), the prepared LiYF4:20%Yb3+, 2%Ho3+, 8%Ce3+ particles are cubic with size of about 1.3 μm, and the small black dots attached to the surface might be considered as CDs. From the HRTEM image in Fig. 2(b), the distance between two adjacent crystal planes is calculated to be about 0.30 nm and 0.25 nm, respectively, corresponding to the (112) crystal plane of tetragonal LiYF4 and (020) crystal plane of CDs [7,14,17,18]. The results show that LiYF4/CDs composite luminescent material was successfully prepared by one-step hydrothermal method. EDS element mapping in Fig. 2(c) shows that the particles contain Li, Y, F components belonging to LiYF4 and contain C element from CDs, confirming the distribution of CDs on the surface of LiYF4 particles. In addition, the presence of Yb3+, Ho3+ and Ce3+ also proves the successful doping of the rare earth ions.

The XPS overall scanning spectrum of LiYF4:Yb,Ho,Ce@CDs is show in Fig. 3 (a), further confirming the chemical composition of the particles. The peaks at 885 eV, 187 eV and 158 eV correspond to the energy spectra of Ce 3d5, Yb 4d5 and Ho 4d5, respectively, which proves that Ce, Yb and Ho elements are successfully doped into LiYF4 particles. In addition, the high-resolution spectrum of C 1s in Fig. 3(b) can be fitted into three major binding energy peaks at 284.9 eV, 285.3 eV and 288.7 eV, which correspond to carbon (C–C or CO), C–O bond and sp3 hybrid CO bond of graphene, respectively. The high-resolution spectrum of O 1s in Fig. 3(c) can be fitted into two major binding energy peaks at 532 eV and 533.1 eV, corresponding to C–O bonds and CO bonds, respectively.

The FT-IR spectrum in Fig. 4 shows the surface functional groups of LiYF4:Yb,Ho,Ce before and after recombination with CDs. The stretching vibration of O–H resulted in a wider absorption peak at 3050-3600 cm−1, while the absorption peaks at 2750-3000 cm−1 and 1450 cm−1 were attributed to the stretching and bending vibrations of C–H. The appearance of a characteristic peak at 1650 cm−1 confirms the existence of CO. By comparing the surface functional groups of LiYF4:Yb,Ho,Ce and CDs before and after composite, the absorption peaks of O–H and C–H groups after composite were significantly enhanced, further indicating the existence of CDs [19–21].

The emission spectra of LiYF4: Yb, Ho, X%Ce (X = 0, 4, 8, 12, 16) @CDs excited by steady-state laser with 980 nm at the power of 20W/cm−1 are shown in Fig. 5 (a). Point I–V in Fig. 5(b) correspond to the CIE coordinates of LiYF4: Yb, Ho, X% Ce (X = 0, 4, 8, 12, 16) @CDs respectively. From Fig. 5 (a), we can find that there are sharp emission peaks at 525–555 nm and 630–660 nm, which are attributed to the 5F4/5S2→5I8 and 5F5→5I8 transitions of Ho3+ ions, respectively. Under 980 nm light excitation, the 2F7/2 level electrons of Yb3+ ion reach the 2F5/2 excited state through the ground state absorption process. Subsequently, the 5I6, 5F5 and 5S2/5F4 electrons of Ho3+ ions are filled by the energy transfer (ET) process from Yb3+ to Ho3+ ions. When the excited state 5S2/5F4 and 5F5 electron radiation of Ho3+ ion attenuates to the ground state 5F8, green and red upconversion emission can be generated. The specific energy transfer diagram is shown in Fig. 6 [22,23]. We can also find that the emission peak intensity near 550 nm, which belongs to green emission, decreases with the increase of Ce3+ ion doping concentration. The fluorescence emission intensity mainly depends on the competition between radiation relaxation and non-radiation relaxation. According to the literature [24–26], with the introduction of Ce3+ ions, two cross relaxation (CR) processes of excited states (5F5 and 5F7) are generated, namely: 5S2/5F4 (Ho3+)+2F5/2 (Ce3+)→5F5 (Ho3+)+2F7/2 (Ce3+) and 5I6 (Ho3+)+2F5/2 (Ce3+)→5I7 (Ho3+)+2F7/2 (Ce3+). The energy transfer upconversion (ETU) from 5I7 to 5F5 can make further contributions to the 5F5 energy level, which ultimately leads to the gradual change of the luminescence color from green to red with the increase of Ce3+ ion doping concentration.

The excitation spectra of LiYF4:Yb3+/Ho3+/Ce3+ (20/2/8%)@CDs composite under 449 nm light monitoring and the emission spectra under 365 nm light excitation are shown in Fig. 7 (a, b). The wide peaks of 300–420 nm in the excitation spectrum originate from the Oπ*→Cπ transition of functional groups in CDs [27,28]. The sample was excited with 365 nm ultraviolet light, and a broad emission peak of CDs was obtained at 455 nm. The luminescence process of CDs is shown in Fig. 7 (c). After absorbing the energy of ultraviolet light, the electrons transition from the Cπ level to the Cπ* level, and then undergo the Cπ* → Nπ* and Nπ* → Oπ* transitions. The electrons relax to the lowest energy level, and finally release the energy to the Cπ level in the form of emitting blue light [18,29,30].

The luminescence photos of clover anti-counterfeiting pattern under 365 nm and 980 nm excitation are shown in Fig. 8 . The UV curable adhesive was mixed with the prepared phosphor, and the mass ratio of powder to adhesive was about 1:1. After mixing evenly, 100 mesh custom screen was used for pattern printing, which was printed on PMMA board. The three leaves counterclockwise from 12 o'clock were smeared with LiYF4: 20%Yb3+,2% Ho3+@CDs; LiYF4: 20%Yb3+,2%Ho3+,4%Ce3+@CDs and LiYF4:20%Yb3+,2%Ho3+,16%Ce3+@CDs phosphors. After being irradiated by UV lamp for 1min, it can be solidified into clover anti-counterfeiting pattern. Under 365 nm excitation, the clover appears blue. Under the excitation of 980 nm near-infrared light, the three leaves of clover appear green, yellow and red colors respectively, achieving dual-mode luminescence anti-counterfeiting effect. By using different concentrations of Ce3+ ion doping ratios, more different colors will appear, which can effectively improve the anti-counterfeiting effect.

In conclusion, we successfully prepared LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+@CDs composite by one-step hydrothermal method, which greatly simplified the preparation steps of up conversion/down transfer composite luminescent materials. By controlling the Ce3+ ion doping concentration, the gradual change of up conversion green light to red light emission of LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+ microcrystals was realized. The composite of rare earth upconversion microcrystals and CDs have different luminescence responses under 365 nm and 985 nm light excitation, respectively. Using the dual wavelength excitation effect of CDs and LiYF4:Yb3+,Ho3+,Ce3+, a dual-mode luminescence anti-counterfeiting clover pattern was designed and obtained. The composites and one-step synthesis technology can provide reference for the preparation of new, simple and environmentally friendly fluorescent anti-counterfeiting materials.

Jiantao Lü: Writing – review & editing, Project administration. Jie Liang: Writing – original draft, Investigation, Data curation. Ting Fan: Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Funding acquisition. Yayun Zhou: Data curation, Supervision. Tingting Deng: Methodology, Resources. Wenqiang Li: Investigation.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: We declare that we have no financial and personal relationships with other people or organizations that can inappropriately influence our work reported in this manuscript.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 51702051) and Foshan inorganic micro-nano luminescent materials engineering technology research center.
